The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a Chinese [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki, Kagawa]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]] in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a Chinese [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun

The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun

An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a Chinese {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun

Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a Chinese {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun

These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a Chinese {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a Chinese {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Tsuda Kofun cluster | native_name =津田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Tsuda Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|type:landmark_region:JP-37|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|18|02|N|134|14|15|E|}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Tsuda Kofun cluster'''|津田古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2013, with the area under protection expanded in 2014.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/271496|title=富津田古墳群|trans-title= Tsuda Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 31, 2022}} ==Overview== The National Historic Site consist of nine Kofuns that were built from the early Kofun period to the early middle Kofun period in an area of 4.5 kilometers east–west and 3.5 kilometers north–south centered on the coastal area of Tsuda Bay overlooking the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. This group of Kofuns was known in the Edo period, and there are records of [[archaeological excavation]]s from the early twentieth century onwards. The earliest mounds are made from piled stones in a style unique to eastern Shikoku and are orientated to the east; however, later tumuli contain cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and are orientated in a north–south direction. This indicates increasing political and cultural influences from the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]]. The split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi at the Tsuda Kofun cluster was made from volcanic stone produced in this area, and the same volcanic stone sarcophagus have been found in the Kinai, Kibi, and Awa regions, which suggests a strong maritime connection. In the middle of the Kofun period, when the [[:en:Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|Tomita Chausuyama Kofun]], the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}} in Shikoku, appeared inland, the construction of Kofuns in the Tsuda site stopped. This clearly shows that a major political consolidation that occurred in the region during that period. {| class="wikitable" style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; font-size: 95%;" |+List of Kofun in the Tsuda Kofun Cluster ! style="width:15em" |name ! style="width:7em" |type ! style="width:4em" |Length !Comments |- |Unobeyama Kofun
(うのべ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The anterior part spreads into a plectrum shape. The mound structure is a stone mound characteristic of eastern Shikoku. Although the burial facilities have not been investigated, judging from the age of the earthenware excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, making it one of the oldest Kofuns in Kagawa Prefecture. |- |Kawahigashi Kofun
(川東古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |37m |The tumulus is built with piled stones, with two burial pits on the top of the mound, but details are unknown. From a small piece of jar-shaped ''haniwa'', it is estimated that it was built in the first half of the 4th century. |- |Koeda Kofun
(古枝古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |34m |The anterior part opens in a plectrum shape. An excavation was conducted in 1962, finding one pit-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and one wooden coffin orientated east–west. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a Chinese {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} and beads from the burial chamber, and a bronze mirror, beads, and iron arrowhead from the wooden coffin. |- |Sekiyama Kofun
(赤山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |45～51m |Currently, only the round part of the tumulus remains, but it was originally a 50-meter keyhole-shaped tumulus. Two split bamboo-shaped sarcophagi are exposed on the top of the mound, one facing east–west and the other facing north–south.The existence of a pit-style stone burial chamber is mentioned in documents, and bronze mirrors and bracelet-shaped stone products are said to have been excavated. Based on the dates of the cylindrical ''haniwa'' and other excavated items, it was built in the first half of the 4th century |- |Hitotsuyama Kofun
(一つ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25～27m |An elliptical tumulus measuring 27 meters north–south by 25 meters east–west, a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was buried in the north–south direction. Judging from the age of the pot-shaped ''haniwa'' excavated from the mound, it is believed to have been constructed in the middle of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.4 Kofun
(岩崎山4号墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |The largest tumulus in the cluster, it was excavated in 1951, during which time a pit-style stone burial chamber orientated north–south was confirmed at the top of the mound, and a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus was found inside. Bronze mirrors, shell rings, bracelet-shaped stone products, iron swords, copper and iron arrowheads, iron agricultural tools, were found as [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]]. In addition to cylindrical ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'' have been excavated from the mound. Based on these items, it is believed to have been built in the middle of the 4th century |- |Ryuozan Kofun
(龍王山古墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |25m |A pit-style stone burial chamber with a total length of 5.9 meters is exposed on the top of the mound in the north–south direction, and there are records that a bronze mirror, an iron mirror, and an iron sword were excavated in the [[:en:Taisho era|Taisho era]]. A pot-shaped ''haniwa'' and a cylindrical ''haniwa'' were excavated from the mound, and it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century. |- |Keboyama Kofun
(けぼ山古墳) |{{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} |61.8m |Fragments of the lid of a split bamboo-shaped sarcophagus were fund on the top of the posterior round part, and there are records that human bones, bronze mirrors, iron swords, and beads were unearthed in the Taisho era, but details of the burial chamber itself is not clear. A jar-shaped ''haniwa'' was unearthed from the mound, and judging from its age, the tumulus is presumed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century |- |Iwasakiyama No.1 Kofun
(岩崎山1号墳) |{{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |18～19m |Two box-type sarcophagi are placed side by side on the top of the mound in the north–south direction. These were excavated in 1927, and in addition to human bones, iron swords, and iron agricultural tools were unearthed. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' and morning glory-shaped ''haniwa'' have been unearthed from the mound, and based on these, it is presumed the tumulus was built at the end of the 4th century |} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.city.sanuki.kagawa.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/31kofun..pdf Sanuki City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[en:Tsuda Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q107999522}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Sanuki, Kagawa]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]
